model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"Use AdamW with a moderately higher learning rate for faster progress under the fixed 256-step budget, while slightly increasing weight decay to keep early overfitting controlled.","secondary_modes":["topk","caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"Disable scheduling since cosine decay over the short budget hurt validation loss; keep the stronger constant-LR AdamW setup.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Increase early feature capacity with a small width bump to improve representation quality within the same optimization setup and step budget.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Raise classifier capacity to better utilize improved convolutional features, with minimal code complexity and low risk of instability.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Slightly increase step size to accelerate learning in the limited 256-step regime after capacity improvements.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Roll back the LR increase since it degraded validation loss; retain the stronger architecture at the previously better step size.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Slightly reduce regularization to improve fit under the short fixed-step budget now that model capacity has increased.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Restore stronger weight decay because lowering it worsened validation loss in this budgeted regime.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Increase examples processed per optimization step to improve progress within the fixed 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Further increase examples per update to maximize data coverage within the fixed 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"With much larger batch size, increase learning rate to improve optimization speed under the same fixed-step budget.","secondary_modes":["micro","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 384","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Test a larger batch to further increase data coverage per update while staying closer to current runtime than jumping directly to 512.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 384","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Continue increasing batch size to maximize sample throughput per fixed optimization step, which has been the strongest improvement trend so far.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 768","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Probe whether an even larger batch continues the strong trend by improving gradient quality and data coverage per fixed update.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 768","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Revert to batch 512 since increasing to 768 slightly hurt loss and substantially increased runtime.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.7e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"At batch 512, test a modest LR increase to improve optimization speed while staying close to the current stable setting.","secondary_modes":["micro","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.7e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Revert to 1.5e-3 because the higher LR degraded validation loss at batch size 512.","secondary_modes":["micro","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Test a modestly larger classifier head to capitalize on stronger features from large-batch training without changing optimization dynamics.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.4e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"After increasing head size, test a slightly lower LR to improve final-step stability while preserving the strong large-batch regime.","secondary_modes":["layout","indexing"]}
